sections.recommendation:
Adopt resolutions authorizing the City Manager to amend contract no. 32400134 with
Lind Marine, contract no. 32400135 with Silverado Contractors, and contract no.
32400145 with Power Engineering to remove and dispose of derelict and abandoned
vessels at the Berkeley Marina by increasing the contract spending amount by $55,000
each for a total not-to-exceed spending amount of $217,000, and extending the contracts
to September 30, 2026.
sections.fiscal_impacts:
In 2024, the Parks, Recreation, and Waterfront Department received a SAVE grant in the
amount of $50,000 from the California Division of Boating and Waterways (DBW) that
requires a local match of 10% ($5,000). The City has three contractors that are available
to perform the abandoned vessel removal work in a timely manner. Depending on the
City’s needs and the availability of the contractors, the spending authority for each of the
three contracts must be increased by $55,000 to provide flexibility for the City. These
funds, totaling $55,000, will be appropriated as part of the second amendment to the
FY2025 annual appropriations ordinance.
Total
Current Amounts $162,000
Proposed $55,000
Amendments
Total $217,000
sections.current_situation:
The Berkeley Marina requires regular removal of abandoned boats and marine debris
from the harbor waters. Each year, approximately five to ten vessels get abandoned by

their owners at the Berkeley Marina. These vessels occupy slips in the Berkeley Marina
that could otherwise be used to generate dockage revenue for the City. They are a
visual blight on our Marina and harm our reputation. Most often, vessels are abandoned
because the owners lack funds needed for repairs, for dockage fees, or both. Due to the
age and neglect of the vessels, they have no value, are not sea-worthy, and are likely to
sink. There are also times when large marine debris such as broken dock sections need
to be removed from the Marina. Upon execution of these contracts and subsequent task
orders, contractors will be able to safely remove vessels or debris from the Marina, take
them back to their shipyard, and properly dispose of them in accordance with federal
and state regulations.
sections.background:
Responsibility for derelict and abandoned vessels, unfortunately, often falls on public
agencies. For the past 20 years, the Division of Boating and Waterways has provided
grant funding assistance to public agencies to properly remove, store, and dispose of
abandoned, wrecked, or dismantled vessels or any other partially submerged objects
that pose a substantial hazard to navigation, from navigable waterways or adjacent
public property, or private property with the landowner’s consent.
In 2022, the City received a SAVE grant (SAVE 2022) from the California Division of
Boating and Waterways in the amount of $60,000. In 2023, the City received an
additional SAVE grant (SAVE 2023) in the amount of $60,000. In 2024, the City received
an additional SAVE grant (SAVE 2024) in the amount of $50,000. This grant will expire
on September 30, 2026.
On October 27, 2023, the City published a Request for Qualifications (RFQ) seeking
qualified firms or individuals to provide Derelict Vessel/Debris Removal & Disposal
services on an as-needed basis (Specification No. 24-11618-C). On November 21,
2023, the City received Statements of Qualifications (SOQs) from three (3) marine
construction firms. A panel of City staff reviewed the SOQs and selected Lind Marine,
Power Engineering Construction, and Silverado Contractors as the best qualified firms to
meet the City’s needs. The City needs to award some or all of the new SAVE 2024
grant of $55,000 to any of the three current contractors, depending on the City’s needs
and the availability of the contractors. To provide this flexibility, the spending authority
for each of the three contracts must be increased by $55,000.
sections.environmental:
Derelict, abandoned, and sunken vessels pose a navigational and environmental hazard
to the Bay waters at the waters and shoreline of the Berkeley Marina. Removal and
proper disposal of these vessels contributes to the improvement of water quality of the
waters of the Bay.
sections.rationale:
The Parks, Recreation, and Waterfront Department has the primary responsibility for
removing derelict and abandoned vessels from local waters. This contract will allow the

RESOLUTION NO. ##,###-N.S.
AMENDMENT TO CONTRACT 32400134: LIND MARINE FOR AS-NEEDED DERELICT
VESSEL/DEBRIS REMOVAL AND DISPOSAL SERVICES
WHEREAS, each year, approximately five to ten vessels get abandoned by their owners
at the Berkeley Marina because they lack funds to repair the vessels or they lack funds to
pay for their dockage fees. Due to the age and neglect of the vessels, they have no value,
are not sea-worthy, and are likely to sink. These vessels occupy slips in the Berkeley
Marina that could otherwise be used to generate dockage revenue for the City, and these
vessels are a visual blight on our Marina and harm our reputation; and
WHEREAS, on January 16, 2024, by Resolution No. 71,165-N.S. the City Council
authorized Contract No. 32300184 with Lind Marine, LLC in an amount not to exceed
$162,000 for as-needed derelict vessel and debris removal and disposal services through
January 16, 2026;
WHEREAS, the current contract not-to-exceed amount is nearly depleted and the City has
need of Lind Marine’s services through September 30, 2026; and
WHEREAS, additional funding of $55,000 for this on-call contract will be available through
a SAVE 2024 grant (Fund 607) in the amount of $50,000 from the California Division of
Boating and Waterways (DBW) and local match Marina Fund (Fund 608) for the amount
of $5,000. These funds will be appropriated as part of the second amendment to the FY
2025 annual appropriations ordinance.
NOW THEREFORE, BE IT RESOLVED by the Council of the City of Berkeley that the City
Manager or his designee is hereby authorized to amend contract no. 32300184 with Lind
Marine to remove and dispose of derelict and abandoned vessels at the Berkeley Marina
by increasing the contract spending amount by $55,000 for a total not-to-exceed contract
amount of $217,000. This contract period w
